core_mechanism: a drag-coupled two-phase (dispersed-particle + carrier-fluid) instability in which relative drift between the two mutually-coupled phases drives a self-reinforcing, back-reacting concentration feedback that amplifies density perturbations of the dispersed phase into localized high-density clumps
brief_reason: The abstract describes dust grains coupled to gas whose relative motion triggers a vigorous, persistent 'streaming instability' that organizes the dispersed phase into dense elongated clumps with reduced velocity dispersion, i.e. a momentum-exchange-driven clustering instability between two coupled phases.
